# GeoScore 2.1 — Evidence-first SEO and GEO Audit

GeoScore audits public websites for verifiable SEO and generative-engine readiness signals. It supports a bounded site audit and a single-URL audit. Site mode samples at most five HTML pages, including the homepage and representative pages discovered from the site.

## Evidence model

- The primary SEO and GEO Readiness scores use factual checks such as indexability, canonical metadata, semantic structure, schema suitability, entity consistency, authorship, source linkage, content extractability, freshness where applicable, and cross-page consistency.
- Every normalized check reports a status, applicable pages, evidence source, observed evidence, and confidence.
- Unknown, unavailable, error, and not-applicable evidence does not reduce the score. Low coverage can result in no overall score instead of a misleading zero.
- AI visibility simulations are labelled `Predicted`, have zero scoring weight, and are kept separate from the factual readiness score.
- GeoScore does not monitor or verify real citations in ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overview, or other answer engines.

## Fix packs

AI-assisted fix packs can only be generated from a recorded, applicable failed check in a stored audit. A fix pack includes the observed evidence, a constrained draft or technical snippet, validation steps, and a provider-neutral handoff prompt. It does not automatically change or publish the audited website, and it must not invent business facts, prices, services, or authority claims.

## Data sources

GeoScore may use live page fetches, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, PageSpeed Insights, Chrome UX Report data, W3C Nu, Common Crawl, public DNS/RDAP data, and other optional public evidence sources. An optional source failure is reported as unavailable evidence and does not lower the factual score.

## Product facts

- Audit modes: site and URL
- Maximum site sample: five HTML pages
- Interface languages: English and Chinese
- License: MIT
- Source: https://github.com/Amiyadesi/geoscore
- Website: https://geo.sayori.org

Current runtime limits and version information are exposed by the public `/api/meta` endpoint so clients do not need to rely on static claims.
